site.title: BAF Camp 2026 – Belgian Activism Festi-Camp | Grupont, 20-23 August
site.description: BAF Camp is a 4-day Belgian activist festival in Grupont: workshops, concerts, self-management and activism. 2026 edition from 20 to 23 August.
site.keywords: BAF Camp, BAF, Belgian Activism Festi-Camp, activist festival Belgium, activism camp, Grupont 2026

hero.programmeValue: Workshops, round-table discussions, concerts, performances & collective moments
hero.objectifValue: Everyone contributes to make this camp happen

enBref.title: In brief
enBref.card1Text: Speakers and artists in several spaces during 4 days of struggle and celebration.
enBref.card2Text: Campsite open from Monday 17th in the evening to come and help set up camp. Possibility of sleeping on site until August 26 (end of dismantling).
enBref.card3Text: Whoever you are, you can join our team by

contact.title: A question?
contact.text: Our volunteer organizing team is available to answer all your questions.

footer.copy: 2026 BAF Camp — by and for everyone

hero.title: Everything you need to know
hero.subtitle: How to get here, where to sleep and what to eat — everything you need to know for the BAF Camp.

infos.construction: Site under construction — complete and final information will be announced soon!

train.title: Train
train.text: The site is 300 m walk from Grupont train station

velo.title: Bike
velo.text: Possible to reach the campsite by bike. Parking and repair equipment on site.

voiture.title: Car
voiture.text: Direct access to the site by car possible at the address: Chem. de Lavaux 1, 6927 Tellin, Belgium. Free parking on site, carpooling encouraged.

restauration.title: Catering
restauration.html: Breakfast, lunch and dinner will be provided by an action kitchen from Gent and Brugge, on a pay-what-you-can basis - bring cash! All meals will be vegan and as many ingredients as possible collected through recup or from local farmers. There will also be a bar serving both soft and alcoholic drinks.

camping.title: Camping
camping.html: The campsite is open from August 17 to 26, on a grassy area a stone's throw from the camp site.
camping.mixiteText: A seperate campsite is reserved exclusively for women, intersex, non-binary and trans people.

dates.title: Opening dates
dates.text: Arrival possible from Sunday August 16 from 2 p.m. Departure no later than Tuesday August 26 at 12 p.m.

bambinerie.title: Bambinerie
bambinerie.introHtml: The BAF Camp, it’s also for families! A dedicated space for children between 4 and 16 years old is planned on the site, so that parents can enjoy the conferences and workshops with peace of mind.

morning.title: Morning slot
morning.html: We offer a 2 hour window in the morning to drop off the children at the Bambinerie, while they attend the program's conferences and workshops.

afternoon.title: Afternoon slot
afternoon.html: We offer a second slot of 2 hours in the afternoon to allow parents to fully enjoy the day.

activities.title: Activities for toddlers
activities.html: Outside of childcare slots, certain organized activities can be adapted for toddlers: games, creations, discoveries, etc. BAF Camp, it’s for everyone!

contact.title: Questions about the Bambinerie?
contact.prefix: Do not hesitate to contact us at

hero.title: Pay-what-you-can
hero.subtitle: So that you contribute according to your means.

intro.title: What is pay-what-you-can?
intro.p1: Tickets for the BAF Camp do not have a fixed price. Everyone contributes according to their real means — not just their salary, but their assets, privileges and safety nets. Those who are more financially comfortable pay more to allow those less privileged to attend.
intro.p2: Pay-what-you-can doesn't always work well — people with more means tend to underpay. That's why we invite you to reflect honestly on your situation before choosing your amount.
intro.p3: Of course, every situation is different: if you regularly send money to loved ones or have significant healthcare expenses, take that into account. Our Privilege-o-meter below can help you calculate a price that is fair to your situation.
intro.p4: This festival is a first edition. If we don't cover costs, the project will go into debt and undermine the possibility of future editions. We really count on your generosity and honesty for this project to be a success, especially financially. Any surplus will be redistributed to various (local) struggles, redistributed via the Fondation Marius Jacob<sup>*</sup> and Mycélium<sup>**</sup>) or used to finance next year's edition.
intro.footnote1Text: is a collective, self-managed financial tool that redistributes grants to activists, collectives and social movements in Belgium — and hosts our bank account.
intro.footnote2Text: is a participatory private foundation that financially supports militant and alternative initiatives in Belgium through its network of patrons.

privilegeMeter.title: The Privilege-o-meter — calculate your fair contribution
privilegeMeter.description: Answer the questions below to estimate where you stand economically. This is not a judgement — it's a tool to help you contribute fairly.

plusItems.i0: Your parents own a property in which you grew up.
plusItems.i2: Your parents have the means to help you buy a property.
plusItems.i3: You have received or will receive an inheritance of more than €50,000.
plusItems.i4: You have at least €5,000 in savings.
plusItems.i5: Your parents can support you in case of financial need without it costing them much.
plusItems.i7: You could get a job or professional help through your parents or their network.
plusItems.i8: You go to restaurants, the cinema or cultural activities.
plusItems.i9: You have hobbies such as: music lessons, drawing, hockey, horse riding, tennis, climbing.
plusItems.i10: You have a permanent contract earning more than €1,800 and no dependants.

minusItems.i0: Your parents are afraid of the police / the army.
minusItems.i1: In case of trouble, your parents do not call the police.
minusItems.i2: Your financial support figures had short, precarious contracts / blue-collar jobs.
minusItems.i3: You had to share a room with siblings for financial reasons.
minusItems.i4: Your financial support figure did not finish secondary school.
minusItems.i5: Your grandparents helped with childcare for financial reasons.
minusItems.i7: You have costs related to repression (fines, legal fees,…).
minusItems.i8: You are a student and your parents do not support you (sufficiently).

recoHigh.title: Solidarity rate.
recoHigh.description: You live comfortably: regular leisure, holidays, salary above €1,800 per month, or a prospect of home ownership. You can contribute more for those who cannot.

recoMid.title: Cost price.
recoMid.description: You have a stable income and make ends meet without too much stress. Your salary is around €1,200–1,600 (with no dependants). You might have a bit of savings, or parents to fall back on if truly needed.

recoLow.title: Reduced price.
recoLow.description: You have no assets and no fixed income that gives you a stable daily life. You regularly rely on the solidarity of those around you.

privilegeMeter.formulaNote:
If you score 8 points or more on the Privilege-o-meter → solidarity rate.
Between 4 and 7 points → cost price.
Fewer than 4 points → reduced rate. Feel free to reach out if you're unsure.

pricing.title: Indicative prices

tierSupport.description: You live comfortably: regular leisure, holidays, a salary above €1,800, or the prospect of home ownership. You are financially well-supported and can afford to contribute more for those who cannot.

tierCost.description: You have a stable income and make ends meet without too much stress. Your salary is around €1,200–€1,600 (with no dependants). You might have a bit of savings, or parents to fall back on if truly needed.

tierReduced.description: You have little or no assets and no fixed income that makes a stable daily life possible. You regularly rely on the solidarity of those around you.

pricing.campingText: If you plan to sleep on site, you can add a participation of ± €5 / day to contribute to the restoration of the venue after the camp.

registration.title: How to register?
registration.description: Registration is done via the online registration form.
registration.stepText: Fill in the registration form to let us know you're coming to the camp.

contact.title: A question?
contact.text: For any question about ticketing, the pay-what-you-can model or financial accessibility, write to us directly:

hero.title: How to fight together?
hero.subtitle: The program will revolve around this question.

programme.construction: Site under construction — the full program will be announced soon!

intro.title: Let’s build the program together!
intro.sentence: The camp will explore two concepts:

together.title: Together
together.tagline: Locate ourselves and find our common ground
together.explanation: What do we have in common? How can we improve the inclusiveness of our struggles and make ourselves more accessible? How can we make our disagreements visible and find points of agreement around which we can rally for mutual benefits? How can we emancipate and not oppress each other within our struggles, that is be truly anti-racist, feminist, anti-capitalist and anti-ableist?

struggle.title: Struggling
struggle.explanation: How can we support each other? What subjects are common and what are the places of struggle near us? What strengths and specificities can we mobilise, where and how? What can we learn from our experiences, strategies and tactics? How can we create a powerful, effective and collective logistical network for our struggles?

filRouge.title: Red thread of programming
filRouge.text: Discover the ideas that will liven up the camp this year.

dayContent.i0: After a week of setting up together, the camp opened around midday.
dayContent.i1:
The two main days of our camp are opportunities for sharing, discussion and workshops centred on the question: ‘How can we fight together?’ – by everyone, for everyone!
If you have a workshop or a stand to suggest, a project to launch, or a topic for a talk to propose - Please feel free to fill in our call for contributions 👊.
Let’s put the programme together!
dayContent.i2:
The two main days of our camp are opportunities for sharing, discussion and workshops centred on the question: ‘How can we fight together?’ – by everyone, for everyone!
If you have a workshop or a stand to suggest, a project to launch, or a topic for a talk to propose - Please feel free to fill in our call for contributions 👊.
Let’s put the programme together!
dayContent.i3: A review of the festival and suggestions for future editions. What are we taking away with us? What’s left behind? The festival wraps up around midday! Then it’s time for three days of collective dismantling and tidying up!

i0.title: Marquees
i0.text: Large spaces for conferences and debates for up to 150 people

i1.title: Workshop tents
i1.text: Friendly spaces to host activities and workshops in small groups around specific issues.

i2.title: Permanent infrastructure
i2.text: Places to meet, discuss and hang out together.

i3.title: The stream
i3.text: Body or brain overheating? There’s a stream where you can swim to cool off!

hero.title: Help us
hero.subtitle: Make a donation, share the event or lend equipment to succeed together.

rejoindre.title: Join us!
rejoindre.text: This camp will be the sum (or more!) of the people who compose it, so come and do your part.No initial skill or legitimacy is required to help and we will try to accomodate any needs you might have. Here are the 3 possible degrees of investment:

i0.title: Become a volunteer
i0.text: Take time at your camp to help life run smoothly there. If everyone gives 2 hours / day all shifts will be full!

i1.title: Help set up and dismantle the camp
i1.text: Yes! The camp will actually be a little longer than announced: assembly begins on August 16 and dismantling continues until August 26.

i2.title: Join a GT
i2.text: Join the organization by joining a working group now to help prepare and shape the camp in advance.

don.title: Make a donation
don.text: Because we want to create an accessible space, there will be no restrictions on access other than the number of places available at the camp. However, despite the work of hundreds of volunteers, some expenses remain unavoidable. Your donation, no matter how small, helps us remain independent and stay true to our values.

bankTransfer.title: Bank transfer
bankTransfer.text: Make a bank transfer to the account of the "Marius Jacob Foundation":
bankTransfer.note: Please use BAF Donation as the payment reference.

qrCode.title: QR Code (bank transfer)
qrCode.text: Scan the QR code with your banking app:
qrCode.note: Compatible with all Belgian banking apps. Please use BAF Donation as the payment reference.

partager.title: Share the event
partager.text: Help us make some noise! Download, print and distribute our communication materials in your neighborhoods, schools and favorite places.

i0.title: A3 poster
i0.text: To print and stick in your favorite places.

i2.title: Stickers
i2.text: Stick it to the system wherever you go.

materiel.title: Equipment loan
materiel.text: Do you have equipment (sound system, lighting, tent, barriers, etc.) and would like to lend it to us for the duration of the festival? Let us know!
materiel.contactText: Contact us at the following address:
materiel.subject: Equipment loan
materiel.subjectWarning: Important: Please put “Equipment loan” in the subject of your email.

hero.title: Self-management
hero.subtitle: Some information on how we work

structure.title: Our structure
structure.text: The BAF Camp is a self-managed event. We organise horizontally, with working groups for different aspects (Logistics, Care, Comms, ...) and a coordination working group. Decisions are made by consent and we advocate an anti-authoritarian attitude. The organizing group is made up of people learning together to create these kinds of events, so it is open to anyone. No skills are required.

gt.title: Working groups
gt.intro: Here is the list of the different working groups ("groupes de travails" or GTs in French), send an email by clicking on the button above if you wish to join one of them.

i0.title: GT Care
i0.text: Research and put into practice processes to fight oppressive practices during the organization and the festival itself.

i1.title: GT Communication
i1.text: Manage social networks, website, external communication of the event.

i3.title: GT Logistics
i3.text: Plan the necessary equipment for the festival as well as the logistics of the site (tent locations, setup and dismantling, etc.)

i4.title: GT Programming
i4.text: Propose workshop themes for the festival programme and contact these collectives or people, collect contributions for the programme.

i5.title: GT Money & Legal
i5.text: Define a budget for the festival, contact donors, think about the legal protection of the festival and request the necessary authorizations.

i6.title: GT Coordination
i6.text: Supports other groups and ensures fluid communication between the different GTs.

benevolat.title: Volunteering
benevolat.text1: On site, our goal is for this event to run in as horizontal and self-managed a way as possible. That’s why, in order to blur the line between volunteers & “consumers” of the festival as much as possible, ideally, all participants give a bit of their time to help run this festival. We’ve calculated that if all volunteers work 2 hours a day, the festival should be able to run in a self-managed way. Furthermore, any task can be adapted if you have a disability of any kind.
benevolat.text2: Feel free to already let us know your volunteering preferences by filling out the registration form. Otherwise, you’ll be able to find tasks to do on the spot in real time! If you have any questions or comments before August 20th, contact us via our email: volontairesBAF@proton.me!
benevolat.text3: The camp will be set up and taken down collectively. We’ll need help! We’ll need to put up marquees and stages, but also create the decor, imagine spaces and build infrastructure out of pallets according to our ideas! Lots of things are still left to invent, so come take part in creating this space starting August 16th! Sign up to help set up the camp beforehand in the form below 👇.
benevolat.text4: But before anything else, don’t forget to get your spot for the event.

items.i0: Come from August 16 to help set up camp
items.i5: Care and active listening (training will be given)

inscription.title: Registration for setup/teardown
inscription.text: Do you already feel like getting involved in the camp and helping make this event happen? It would be super valuable for us if you filled out the form below, so we can already get an idea of our numbers. We’ll get back to you in August once we’ve received your message.
inscription.deadline: Registration deadline via the form: July 31st.

hero.title: Our charter
hero.subtitle: The political and strategic bases of the BAF Camp

pourquoi.title: Why this camp
pourquoi.p1: The BAF Camp is a space to meet, organize and bolster our struggles. It starts from an observation: our rights are declining, repression is increasing and our movements are fragmented.
pourquoi.p2: The objective of the BAF camp is to create links between collectives, share practices and build strong and lasting alliances, while maintaining lively and accessible spaces.

objectifs.title: What we are trying to do

i0.title: Connections
i0.text: Create bridges between struggles and territories (Brussels, Wallonia, Flanders).

i2.title: Get organized
i2.text: Share strategies, debate and find common ground.

positionnement.title: Positioning
positionnement.text: The camp is part of social, anti-fascist, ecological, peasant, feminist, anti-racist, anti-imperialism, decolonial, anti-validist and queer struggles. It is based on an anti-authoritarian approach and seeks to limit relationships of domination, including internally.

items.i0: Refusal of oppression (racism, sexism, validism, classism, LGBTQIA+phobias, etc.)
items.i1: Pay attention to power relations in discussions
items.i2: Those concerned by an issue are put centre stage
items.i3: No emphasis on a particular organization or ideology

fonctionnement.title: Collective operation
fonctionnement.text: The camp is self-managed and based on active participation. It operates through working groups and decisions taken collectively.

items.i0: Horizontal organization based on consensual decision making
items.i1: Everyone participates through volunteer shifts during the camp
items.i2: Openness to new people and groups

cadre.title: Basic framework
cadre.text: The camp is not a guaranteed “safe” space, but a space where we try to build conditions of respect and listening.

items.i0: Respect for others and their experiences
items.i1: Consent (when talking to others, taking photos, physical contact...)
items.i2: Refusal of violent or manipulative behavior
items.i3: Take your share of responsibility in the event of conflict

cadre.note: A team dedicated to care and conflict management will be present during the camp.

accessibilite.title: Accessibility & inclusion
accessibilite.text: Work is being carried out to make the camp accessible to as many people as possible, even if everything cannot be perfect.

items.i0: Interpretation into French, Dutch and English as much as possible
items.i1: Pay attention to specific needs (food, accessibility, etc.)
items.i2: Intentionally diverse spaces (e.g. womxn only activities)
items.i3: Working with relevant groups in the run up to the festival

charteComplete.title: Complete charter
charteComplete.text: This page is a summary. The complete charter details the rules, definitions and processes. Its reading is strongly recommended for participating in the camp.

hero.title: Financing
hero.subtitle: How the BAF Camp is funded and the presentation of our budget in complete transparency.

financements.construction: Site under construction — complete and final information will be announced soon!

sources.title: Funding sources
sources.text: The camp is self-funded. The vast majority of the festival is funded by participants. The majority of financial sources will come from free-price ticket sales, as well as on-site purchases. For that :
sources.cashSubtitle: There are no cash machines close to the site.
sources.subnote: Here’s how we plan to fairly achieve a balanced and sustainable budget:

i0.title: Ticketing
i0.text: Ticket sales represent the festival’s main source of income. The price is pay-what-you-can, with a privilege-o-meter to facilitate conscious and informed free pricing.

i1.title: Bar & Food
i1.text: Revenue from the bar contributes to the events budget. Revenues from food go to the collective(s) running the canteen.

i2.title: Profits?
i2.text: Profits from the event will be used for current or future struggles.

transparence.title: Budget transparency
transparence.text: The festival accounts will be accessible to everyone. The results will be presented after the edition.

items.i1: Reinjection of profits into selected projects

soutenir.title: Support us
soutenir.text: Do you want to become a partner or contribute to the project? You can click on the link below or contact us via our email.
